L1.area: Network connectivity and remote server communication

L2.description: The agent's TLS implementation fails to verify the hostname of the server's SSL certificate during connection establishment. Although the certificate chain may be validated against trusted certificate authorities, the absence of hostname verification allows an attacker who possesses a valid certificate for any domain to perform man-in-the-middle attacks on encrypted communications.

L3.area: TLS/SSL connection setup and certificate verification within the agent's HTTP client layer
L3.description: The agent's TLS implementation fails to verify the hostname of the server's SSL certificate when establishing HTTPS connections. In multiple code paths used for certificate validation — including both the native Python SSL wrapper and an external certificate verification callout — only the certificate chain is checked against trusted roots while hostname verification is omitted. An attacker with a valid certificate for any domain can intercept encrypted communications via a man-in-the-middle attack.

ground_truth.reason: The Scalyr Agent before 2.1.10 does not verify the server hostname during SSL/TLS connections, allowing man-in-the-middle attacks even when certificate chain validation is enabled.
